summary: Heavily obfuscated bulk email/SMS tool with red-team framing
details: Package self-describes as an 'Advanced email sender' tagged 'red-team' and ships ~45 heavily obfuscated JavaScript files under.ad/ implementing bulk SMS via SMTP-to-SMS gateways and Twilio, OAuth2 token acquisition for Microsoft Graph and Gmail, contact extraction from authenticated mailboxes (extractContacts, extractContactsIMAP), DKIM/SPF key generation, and a web command panel. The reachable behavior is gated behind CLI subcommands the operator invokes after installation; the package does not read installer-side secrets (no ~/.aws, ~/.ssh, ~/.npmrc, browser profile, env-var scraping), does not fetch and execute remote payloads at install or import time, and does not relay caller-supplied data to a hardcoded author endpoint. The postinstall script only writes a Windows.cmd shim into the npm global bin directory pointing at the package's own.ad/x0.js entry — the documented bin-shim workaround for a bin entry that resolves into a dotfile directory. Heavy obfuscation of every reachable source file is unusual for a legitimate operator tool and is a strong transparency / abuse-tooling signal, and the package's stated purpose (mass SMS/email with mailbox harvesting) is consistent with phishing/spam infrastructure aimed at third parties — but the harm is to the operator's targets, not to the installer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
